title: Coryphantha ottonis
intro: Coryphantha ottonis, also known as the Otton's pincushion cactus, is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Coryphantha ottonis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coryphantha ottonis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Coryphantha ottonis is classified within the family Cactaceae and the subfamily Cactoideae. This species is further grouped into the tribe Cacteae and the genus Coryphantha. The genus Coryphantha is a group of small to medium-sized cacti native to Central and North America. These plants are commonly known as beehive cacti due to their unique, globular shape resembling beehives. Coryphantha ottonis is a species within this group, specifically characterized by its distinctive morphology and distribution.
uses: Coryphantha ottonis is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Mexico, where it is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to be used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides, which are thought to have antibacterial and antifungal activities. In folk medicine, the plant is often applied topically or ingested in small amounts to promote healing and reduce inflammation. More research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and risks of using Coryphantha ottonis as a medicinal plant.

properties: Coryphantha ottonis, also known as the "Pincushion Cactus," has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Research suggests that Coryphantha ottonis may have wound-healing properties, due to its ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and promote tissue repair. Additionally, its extracts have been found to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which could help regulate the immune system. Further studies are needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Coryphantha ottonis and its applications in medicine.
constituents: Coryphantha ottonis contains various active constituents, including alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides. These compounds are believed to be responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The specific alkaloids present in Coryphantha ottonis have been identified as ottonisidine and ottonine. These alkaloids are thought to possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of these active constituents on human health.

side_effects: Coryphantha ottonis is a type of cactus, and like all cacti, it is not typically considered for human consumption due to potential side effects.
